name: Motorbike (Tutorial)
description: Stock OpenFOAM tutorial: external aerodynamics of a motorcycle using snappyHexMesh. Full meshing + solve pipeline. A thorough setup verification for complex geometry cases.

mesh.title: Generate Mesh
mesh.description: Three-step meshing: surfaceFeatureExtract, blockMesh, then snappyHexMesh. The motorcycle geometry is in constant/triSurface/.
mesh.tip: snappyHexMesh reads the motorcycle geometry from constant/triSurface/motorBike.obj.gz.

boundaries.title: Boundary Conditions
boundaries.description: Inlet at 20 m/s, outlet at zero-gradient, k-omega SST turbulence model.
boundaries.tip: The inlet uses fixedValue for U (20 m/s in X direction) and calculated for turbulence fields.

solver.title: Solver Settings
solver.description: SIMPLE algorithm with k-omega SST turbulence. 500 iterations.
solver.tip: For a quick test, reduce endTime to 100 iterations.

run.title: Run Simulation
run.description: Execute simpleFoam. This case takes longer than the 2D airfoil due to the 3D mesh.
run.tip: If this completes, your setup handles complex geometry, meshing, and parallel solving.

postprocess.title: View Results
postprocess.description: Visualize pressure distribution, velocity field, and force coefficients on the motorcycle.
postprocess.tip: In ParaView, apply 'Extract Block' to isolate the motorcycle surfaces, then color by pressure.
